proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher mean objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a higher mean objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_sotorasib.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher mean objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a higher mean objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_osimertinib.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.174 on treatment_pembrolizumab vs 0.164 off; logit OR=1.073, p=0.00329.
Response rate 0.169 on treatment_sotorasib vs 0.169 off; logit OR=1.006, p=0.82.
Response rate 0.170 on treatment_olaparib vs 0.169 off; logit OR=1.010, p=0.714.
Response rate 0.165 on treatment_osimertinib vs 0.170 off; logit OR=0.965, p=0.175.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Patients with stage_iv = 1 have lower probability of objective_response than those with stage_iv = 0.
Patients with has_brain_mets = 1 have lower probability of objective_response than those without.
Older age_years is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different probability of objective_response than male patients.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression of objective_response on ecog_ps: OR=0.687, p=1.73e-93.
Logistic regression of objective_response on stage_iv: OR=0.744, p=1.31e-33.
Logistic regression of objective_response on has_brain_mets: OR=0.777, p=2.04e-18.
Logistic regression of objective_response on age_years: OR=1.001, p=0.668.
Logistic regression of objective_response on sex_female: OR=1.086, p=0.000566.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with tmb_high = 1 have a higher probability of objective_response than tmb_high = 0.
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation, stk11_mutation, keap1_mutation, and tp53_mutation each have a non-zero main effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response 0.182 in tmb_high=1 vs 0.164 in tmb_high=0; OR=1.136, p=9.83e-07.
pdl1_tps continuous OR=1.180, p=0.00319 per unit (i.e. 0->1).
egfr_mutation: OR=0.969, p=0.376; rates pos 0.165 vs neg 0.170.
kras_g12c: OR=1.003, p=0.938; rates pos 0.169 vs neg 0.169.
alk_fusion: OR=0.960, p=0.464; rates pos 0.164 vs neg 0.169.
brca2_mutation: OR=0.949, p=0.46; rates pos 0.162 vs neg 0.169.
stk11_mutation: OR=0.970, p=0.37; rates pos 0.165 vs neg 0.170.
keap1_mutation: OR=1.006, p=0.852; rates pos 0.170 vs neg 0.169.
tp53_mutation: OR=0.996, p=0.874; rates pos 0.169 vs neg 0.169.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with egfr_mutation = 1, treatment_osimertinib increases objective_response more than it does among egfr_mutation = 0 patients (positive interaction on the log-odds scale).

analyses.result_summary: Cell response rates: osi-/egfr- 0.171, osi-/egfr+ 0.166, osi+/egfr- 0.166, osi+/egfr+ 0.164; interaction OR=1.024, p=0.758. No detectable interaction; osimertinib does not selectively increase response in EGFR-mutant patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with kras_g12c = 1, treatment_sotorasib increases objective_response more than among kras_g12c = 0 patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Cell rates: soto-/kras- 0.169, soto-/kras+ 0.166, soto+/kras- 0.169, soto+/kras+ 0.175; interaction OR=1.061, p=0.427. No statistical interaction detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with brca2_mutation = 1, treatment_olaparib increases objective_response more than among brca2_mutation = 0 patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Cell rates: olap-/brca- 0.169, olap-/brca+ 0.168, olap+/brca- 0.171, olap+/brca+ 0.146; interaction OR=0.837, p=0.275. Point estimate is in the wrong direction (negative) and not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 (high PD-L1) derive a larger increase in objective_response from treatment_pembrolizumab than patients with PD-L1 < 0.5 (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_high interaction).
On the continuous pdl1_tps scale, the treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction on objective_response is positive.

analyses.result_summary: pembro-/pdl1lo 0.166, pembro-/pdl1hi 0.156, pembro+/pdl1lo 0.164, pembro+/pdl1hi 0.209; interaction OR=1.457, p=5.04e-11. Strongly positive interaction: pembro lifts response from ~16% to ~21% in PD-L1 high but not in PD-L1 low.
Continuous pdl1_tps x pembrolizumab interaction beta=0.591, p=1.57e-07.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with tmb_high = 1 derive a larger increase in objective_response from treatment_pembrolizumab than patients with tmb_high = 0 (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: pembro-/tmb- 0.163, pembro-/tmb+ 0.167, pembro+/tmb- 0.165, pembro+/tmb+ 0.197; interaction OR=1.202, p=0.000415. Positive interaction: pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in TMB-high tumors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stk11_mutation = 1 derive less benefit (smaller increase in objective_response) from treatment_pembrolizumab than stk11_mutation = 0 patients (negative interaction).
Patients with keap1_mutation = 1 derive less benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab than keap1_mutation = 0 patients (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: pembro-/stk- 0.162, pembro-/stk+ 0.173, pembro+/stk- 0.177, pembro+/stk+ 0.158; interaction OR=0.810, p=0.00178. Negative interaction: STK11 mutation abrogates pembrolizumab response.
pembro-/keap- 0.163, pembro-/keap+ 0.168, pembro+/keap- 0.174, pembro+/keap+ 0.171; interaction OR=0.944, p=0.363. No detectable KEAP1 effect on pembrolizumab response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with alk_fusion = 1 have a larger increase in objective_response from treatment_osimertinib than alk_fusion = 0 patients (positive interaction).
Patients with alk_fusion = 1 derive less benefit (smaller increase in objective_response) from treatment_pembrolizumab than alk_fusion = 0 patients (negative interaction, oncogene-driven biology).

analyses.result_summary: osi-/alk- 0.171, osi-/alk+ 0.164, osi+/alk- 0.166, osi+/alk+ 0.162; interaction OR=1.025, p=0.837. No osimertinib advantage in ALK+ tumors (as expected — osimertinib targets EGFR, not ALK).
pembro x alk_fusion interaction interaction OR=1.014, p=0.9; null result.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher nlr is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Patients with liver_mets = 1 have lower objective_response than those without.
Patients with bone_mets = 1 have lower objective_response than those without.
Patients with adrenal_mets = 1 have lower objective_response than those without.
Patients with pleural_effusion = 1 have lower objective_response than those without.
Patients with pericardial_effusion = 1 have lower objective_response than those without.

analyses.result_summary: liver_mets: rate pos 0.173 vs neg 0.168; OR=1.033, p=0.331.
bone_mets: rate pos 0.173 vs neg 0.168; OR=1.041, p=0.178.
adrenal_mets: rate pos 0.165 vs neg 0.169; OR=0.966, p=0.38.
pleural_effusion: rate pos 0.170 vs neg 0.169; OR=1.005, p=0.879.
pericardial_effusion: rate pos 0.173 vs neg 0.169; OR=1.033, p=0.592.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with squamous histology have a different objective_response than those with adenocarcinoma.
Smoking_status (current vs former vs never) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: squamous response 0.174 vs adeno 0.167; OR=1.048, p=0.0762.
smoking_status=current: rate 0.172 vs other 0.168; OR=1.035, p=0.19.
smoking_status=former: rate 0.168 vs other 0.169; OR=0.993, p=0.77.
smoking_status=never: rate 0.164 vs other 0.170; OR=0.958, p=0.202.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher smoking_pack_years amplifies the objective_response benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab (positive interaction).
Never-smokers (smoking_status='never') derive a smaller objective_response benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab (negative interaction).
Never-smokers derive a larger objective_response benefit from treatment_osimertinib than ever-smokers (positive interaction, EGFR-enriched).

analyses.result_summary: smoking_pack_years x pembrolizumab interaction beta=-0.0011, p=0.27; null.
never-smoker x pembrolizumab interaction interaction OR=0.931, p=0.289; null.
never-smoker x osimertinib interaction OR=0.881, p=0.0919; trend toward negative interaction (p~0.09).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Female patients derive a larger increase in objective_response from treatment_pembrolizumab than male patients (positive interaction).
Female patients derive a different increase in objective_response from treatment_osimertinib than male patients.
Female patients derive a different increase in objective_response from treatment_sotorasib than male patients.
Female patients derive a different increase in objective_response from treatment_olaparib than male patients.

analyses.result_summary: male/no 0.164, female/no 0.165, male/yes 0.164, female/yes 0.186; interaction OR=1.157, p=0.00237.
male/no 0.165, female/no 0.177, male/yes 0.162, female/yes 0.170; interaction OR=0.973, p=0.6.
male/no 0.163, female/no 0.175, male/yes 0.164, female/yes 0.176; interaction OR=1.000, p=0.993.
male/no 0.162, female/no 0.176, male/yes 0.167, female/yes 0.173; interaction OR=0.947, p=0.299.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race/ethnicity is associated with objective_response (each race indicator is tested for a non-zero effect, with white as one comparator).
Asian patients (race_ethnicity='asian') derive a larger increase in objective_response from treatment_osimertinib than non-Asian patients (positive interaction).
Black patients (race_ethnicity='black') derive a different increase in objective_response from treatment_pembrolizumab than non-Black patients.

analyses.result_summary: race=white: in-group 0.165 vs other 0.175; OR=0.932, p=0.00477.
race=black: in-group 0.178 vs other 0.168; OR=1.071, p=0.0563.
race=hispanic: in-group 0.172 vs other 0.168; OR=1.022, p=0.519.
race=asian: in-group 0.177 vs other 0.168; OR=1.064, p=0.213.
race=other: in-group 0.185 vs other 0.169; OR=1.122, p=0.158.
Asian x osimertinib interaction OR=0.982, p=0.872; null.
Black x pembrolizumab interaction OR=1.048, p=0.514; null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured) is associated with objective_response.
rural_residence = 1 is associated with lower objective_response than rural_residence = 0.
Higher education_years is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: insurance=medicare: in-group 0.168 vs other 0.169; OR=0.994, p=0.813.
insurance=private: in-group 0.172 vs other 0.167; OR=1.037, p=0.136.
insurance=medicaid: in-group 0.161 vs other 0.170; OR=0.938, p=0.0662.
insurance=uninsured: in-group 0.170 vs other 0.169; OR=1.008, p=0.897.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Major comorbidities (autoimmune_disease, interstitial_lung_disease_history, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, diabetes_mellitus, hypertension) each have a non-zero main effect on objective_response.
Patients with autoimmune_disease = 1 derive a smaller increase in objective_response from treatment_pembrolizumab than those without (negative interaction).
Patients with interstitial_lung_disease_history = 1 derive a smaller increase in objective_response from treatment_pembrolizumab than those without (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: autoimmune x pembrolizumab interaction OR=0.888, p=0.283; null.
ILD x pembrolizumab interaction OR=0.923, p=0.524; null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with prior_chemotherapy = 1 have lower objective_response than those without.
Higher prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with lower objective_response.
Patients with prior_immunotherapy = 1 derive a smaller increase in objective_response from treatment_pembrolizumab (negative interaction).
Other prior treatments (prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy) and years_since_diagnosis each have a non-zero main effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: prior_immunotherapy x pembrolizumab interaction OR=1.145, p=0.0442; positive (not negative) interaction at borderline significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher pain_nrs is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher cough_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
At least one of the 23 candidate drug-metabolism SNPs (snp_rs*) has a non-zero main effect on objective_response.
At least one drug-metabolism SNP modifies the objective_response benefit of one of the four treatments (non-zero SNP x treatment interaction).
Patients with ecog_ps >= 2 (ecog_high=1) derive a smaller increase in objective_response from treatment_pembrolizumab than ecog_ps <= 1 patients (negative interaction).
Patients with ecog_ps >= 2 derive a different increase in objective_response from treatment_osimertinib than ecog_ps <= 1 patients.
Patients with has_brain_mets = 1 derive a smaller increase in objective_response from treatment_pembrolizumab than those without brain mets (negative interaction).
Patients with has_brain_mets = 1 derive a different increase in objective_response from treatment_osimertinib than those without brain mets.
Adjusting for clinical, biomarker, and laboratory covariates, the treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction remains a positive, statistically significant effect on objective_response.
Adjusting for the same covariates, the treatment_pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation interaction remains a negative effect on objective_response.
After adjustment, ECOG performance status, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and albumin_g_dl remain independently associated with objective_response in the expected directions.
Adjusting for the same covariates, the treatment-by-driver-mutation interactions for osimertinib x egfr_mutation, sotorasib x kras_g12c, and olaparib x brca2_mutation remain non-significant.

analyses.code: logit multivariable model with biomarker x treatment interactions
analyses.result_summary: In adjusted model, treatment_pembrolizumab:pdl1_tps beta=+0.595 (OR=1.813), p=1.59e-07.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab x tmb_high interaction on objective_response is concentrated in patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 (PD-L1 high) and is essentially absent in PD-L1 low patients.

analyses.result_summary: In pdl1_high=0 subgroup (n=39251), pembrolizumab x tmb_high interaction beta=+0.003, p=0.959.
In pdl1_high=1 subgroup (n=10749), pembrolizumab x tmb_high interaction beta=+0.690, p=1.98e-10.
